commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Windows doctor/update service repair to audit stale gateway service versions, restart or activate running managed gateways, migrate embedded gateway auth before task rewrites, preserve legacy update metadata, and remove Startup-folder fallback launchers after Scheduled Task evidence.

PR surface: Source +120, Tests +336. Total +456 across 10 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the blocking review finding from source inspection: when the hidden-launcher env flag is absent, resolveStartupEntryPaths does not include a legacy .vbs path, so removeStartupEntries can leave it behind. I did not run a live Windows reproduction.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can address the remaining hidden-launcher cleanup defect and add the missing regression test before maintainers decide on proof and landing.

target.title: fix(windows): repair doctor update fallback migration
target.body:
## Summary

- fixes Windows update-mode doctor repair so running managed gateways are activated/restarted, while stopped services stay staged
- audits stale `OPENCLAW_SERVICE_VERSION` values and repairs version drift through the existing gateway service doctor path
- removes legacy Windows Startup-folder fallback launchers (`.cmd` and hidden `.vbs`) only after Task Scheduler reports running evidence
- preserves auth during update-mode task rewrites by migrating a legacy embedded `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN` into `gateway.auth.token` before reinstalling the task
- keeps legacy update-parent config handoff readable by preserving `meta.lastTouchedVersion` when the old parent resumes after doctor writes

Fixes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/87156.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-gateway-services.test.ts src/commands/doctor-update.test.ts src/daemon/schtasks.startup-fallback.test.ts src/daemon/schtasks.stop.test.ts` - passed, 74 focused daemon/doctor tests on `18a08eb512d6baba75ff52446e1162b4a5ebb72e`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/commands/doctor-gateway-services.ts src/commands/doctor-gateway-services.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/commands/doctor-service-audit.test-helpers.ts src/commands/doctor-update.test.ts src/commands/doctor-update.ts src/daemon/schtasks.startup-fallback.test.ts src/daemon/schtasks.stop.test.ts src/daemon/schtasks.ts src/daemon/service-audit.ts` - passed
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/commands/doctor-gateway-services.ts src/commands/doctor-gateway-services.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/commands/doctor-service-audit.test-helpers.ts src/commands/doctor-update.test.ts src/commands/doctor-update.ts src/daemon/schtasks.startup-fallback.test.ts src/daemon/schtasks.stop.test.ts src/daemon/schtasks.ts src/daemon/service-audit.ts` - passed
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` - passed
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` - clean after fixing two accepted findings: update-mode embedded token loss and legacy-parent `lastTouchedVersion` drift
- Native Windows proof: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26690553757 passed on GitHub-hosted `windows-2025`, checkout SHA `18a08eb512d6baba75ff52446e1162b4a5ebb72e`; runner reported Microsoft Windows Server 2025 / `Microsoft Windows NT 10.0.26100.0`, x64, PowerShell 7.4.16, Git 2.54.0.windows.1
- WSL probe in the same run found `C:\Windows\system32\wsl.exe`, `Default Version: 2`, and `wsl_status_exit=0`; no distro was installed, so `wsl_exec_exit=-1` and `wsl2_ok=false`
- Direct Crabbox/Testbox proof was attempted but blocked:
  - `blacksmith testbox list --all` required `blacksmith auth login`
  - `../crabbox/bin/crabbox list --provider azure --json` returned stale Windows lease ids, but reusing `cbx_58d8b308dab1` returned coordinator 404
  - fresh Azure Windows run `run_f0d1785e6046` did not allocate a coordinator lease before local cancellation after more than 8 minutes
  - static SSH to the listed Azure host waited on `20.94.194.122:2222` bootstrap auth and never became reachable

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Windows doctor/update migration now distinguishes running vs stopped managed gateways, detects stale service versions, preserves gateway auth during task rewrites, restarts stale installed/running gateways after update, and only removes Startup-folder fallback launchers after Task Scheduler running evidence.

Real environment tested: native Windows Server 2025 through `windows-testbox-probe.yml`; WSL availability probe on the same Windows runner; local focused Vitest shards for daemon startup fallback, daemon stop, doctor update, and doctor gateway services.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `ghx workflow run windows-testbox-probe.yml --ref main -f target_ref=fix/windows-doctor-startup-migration -f runner_label=windows-2025 -f keepalive_minutes=0 -f require_wsl2=false -f import_ubuntu_wsl2=false -f enable_wsl2_features=false`; focused local test/format/lint/autoreview commands listed above.

Evidence after fix: Windows probe run 26690553757 passed and confirmed native Windows execution at `18a08eb512d6baba75ff52446e1162b4a5ebb72e`; local focused tests passed 74 assertions; branch autoreview was clean after the token-preservation and legacy-parent metadata fixes.

Observed result after fix: the patch is validated for native Windows and the service/doctor unit behavior. WSL was available but had no installed distro in the hosted runner, so the patch does not yet have a successful WSL distro boot proof.

What was not tested: a successful WSL2 distro boot inside a Windows runner or direct Crabbox Windows/WSL lease proof; those are blocked by current runner distro provisioning and Crabbox/Blacksmith lease/auth readiness described above.
target.author: vincentkoc

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
